name: Texas White Lightning Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and wash the beans; place them in a large Dutch oven. Cover with water 2 inches above the beans.
2. Soak for 8 hours, then drain the beans and return them to the Dutch oven. Discard the liquid.
3. Add 3 cans of broth and the next seven ingredients (white pepper, oregano, cumin, salt, ground cloves, cooked chicken, canned chopped chiles, water, and jalapeno) to the pot.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 2 hours or until beans are tender, stirring occasionally.
5. Add the remaining can of broth, any leftover cooked chicken, and the next three ingredients (the other canned chopped chiles, water, and jalapeno) to the pot.
6. Cover and continue simmering for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
7. Using kitchen shears, make four cuts in each tortilla toward, but not through, the center.
8. Line serving bowls with tortillas, overlapping the cut edges of each tortilla.
9. Spoon the chili into the bowls and top with shredded Monterey Jack cheese, salsa, and sour cream.
10. Serve immediately. Serves 8
